title: Dimacria punctata
intro:
There is no information found about Dimacria punctata, however I found Dimocarpus punctatus and Dimocarpus longan (also known as Dimocarpus littoralis) and Dimocarpus confusus and another called  Dimocarpus albellus  but most relevant seems to be the one called  Dimocarpus longan  or  Dimocarpus punctatus

Dimocarpus punctatus is a plant species known for its edible fruit, with the plant being used to make various herbal preparations.

The health benefits of Dimocarpus punctata include its use in traditional medicine to treat fever, rheumatism, and as a general tonic to boost the immune system. Its bioactive constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Dimocarpus punctata include teas, infusions, and extracts that are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
